text:
There was a curious frog who loved exploring. One sunny day, while climbing a tree branch, he noticed a strange fluttering around a bright, orange pill. Intrigued, he decided to investigate.

Upon closer inspection, he discovered an antique locket. Curious about its secrets, he twisted it open. Inside, he found a tiny, dusty portrait.

"Mmm," thought the frog, "could this be my great-grandmother?"

He carefully pulled the portrait out and tucked it into the seat of his grassy blanket. Every evening after that, the frog returned to look at the painting.

Years passed like clouds passing by, and the frog became a wise old froggy. His favorite spot was the old tree beside the pond.

Every evening after sunset, he'd return to admire his portrait. In his mind's eye, he could see old Lady Frogmore smiling warmly at him.

One evening, while sipping nectar from a blossomed branch, the frog heard a rustling
